statement: Judge-gym should require replay testing and continue-as-new discipline by default, while treating pinned worker rollouts as conditional rather than universally preferred.
rationale: Replay safety and history control are clearly required, but pinned rollout has enough preview and draining caveats that it should be a deliberate choice per workflow type.
keyAssumptions: The team can maintain representative workflow histories for replay testing
Workflow duration and deploy cadence can be measured well enough to pick pinned versus auto-upgrade
validationCriteria: Workflow-code changes fail CI when replay is unsafe
Each workflow type has an explicit rollout policy and continue-as-new trigger
critiquePoints: Pinned worker versioning carries real version-retention and draining overhead
questions: Which workflow types, if any, are short enough to justify pinned rollout first?
